prompt:
HR platform ticket. When a hire falls through we currently cancel the work relationship in the Oracle Fusion HCM UI by hand, because the last scripted attempt POSTed plain JSON at a guessed URL with a made-up worker ID, treated a 202 like a failure, and printed 'HTTP 400' when the pod explained in two structured messages exactly what was wrong. Build the cancel workflow as a proper C# library in this test project (namespace OracleHcm, files alongside ActionTests.cs; the csproj already compiles everything). ActionTests.cs is the acceptance contract; it, the csproj, .gitignore, and everything under docs/ are protected.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official 'REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM' documentation on docs.oracle.com — at minimum the 'Cancel a work relationship' action operation page, the worker work-relationships child collection page, and the framework's status-code and framework-version pages. Confirm from the docs: the action's exact path shape and the vendor media types on both sides of it, which parameters the action body takes, where a client is supposed to get workersUniqID from (hint: the docs are explicit that you never fabricate it), what 200 vs 201 vs 202 mean in this framework, and how a version-4 structured error document is laid out. First-party Oracle documentation only (docs.oracle.com). docs/contract.json pins our tenant's wire fixture — including how THIS pod signals completion after a 202 (Location header with a syncToken that a rebuilt URL loses, polled until the canceled row is gone); the docs define what 202 means, the fixture defines our tenant's follow-up, and your summary should connect the two. If live docs contradict a pin, report the conflict and leave protected files alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Surface the tests compile against (design beyond it is yours; expect ~4-6 files):
- HcmActionClient(HttpClient http, string baseUrl, string user, string password, int maxStatusPolls = 5) with ResolveWorkerAsync(personNumber) → WorkerRef(PersonId, PersonNumber, WorkersUniqId), ResolveRelationshipAsync(worker, legalEmployerName) → RelationshipRef(Worker, PeriodOfServiceId, LegalEmployerName), and CancelWorkRelationshipAsync(rel, actionCode, reasonCode) → CancelOutcome(Mode 'sync'|'async', Polls, ReturnValue, RawResult).
- Zero resolution matches → InvalidOperationException naming the person number, before any action POST. The action POST is issued exactly once no matter what happens afterwards.
- OracleRestException for structured failures: HttpStatus, Title, ErrorStatus, Details (ordered OracleErrorEntry(Detail, ErrorCode, ErrorPath) — keep every entry). Message carries title + first code; credentials never appear in exception text.
- HcmAsyncTimeoutException with Polls when the 202 follow-up never resolves within maxStatusPolls.
- HcmStatusClassifier.Classify(int) → HcmResponseKind {Ok, Created, Accepted, NoContent, ClientError, ServerError} per the documented status semantics.

Acceptance: dotnet test — all tests green. Loopback mock only, dummy service account, no sleeps, no DateTime.Now.

research.product: Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — cancelWorkRelationship worker action
research.interface: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM (farws), resource version 11.13.18.05, REST framework version 4

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Cancel a work relationship
official_sources.used_for: exact action path /workers/{workersUniqID}/child/workRelationships/{PeriodOfServiceId}/action/cancelWorkRelationship; Content-Type application/vnd.oracle.adf.action+json with REST-Framework-Version 4; actionCode/reasonCode request parameters; 200 response with application/vnd.oracle.adf.actionresult+json body {"returnValue":"true"}

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Get all worker work relationships
official_sources.used_for: workRelationships child collection path; q filtering on LegalEmployerName/WorkerType; PeriodOfServiceId as the relationship identifier; guidance that workersUniqID must be obtained by querying the workers collection, never generated by the client

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Status Codes
official_sources.used_for: documented status semantics driving the classifier and workflow: 200 OK, 201 Created, 202 Accepted (request accepted, processing not complete), 204 No Content, 4xx client errors (400/401/403/404/409/412/428), 5xx server errors

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Setting the REST Framework Version
official_sources.used_for: REST-Framework-Version header; version-4-and-above structured error document application/vnd.oracle.adf.error+json with title, status (string), o:errorDetails[].detail/o:errorCode/o:errorPath

research.language_rationale: The HR workflow automation this joins is a .NET service; Oracle publishes no official .NET SDK for Fusion HCM REST and documents the surface as plain REST with vendor media types, so HttpClient with System.Text.Json against the documented endpoints is the credible integration path and keeps the seed hermetic.
